title: xanthium orientale subsp. saccharatum
intro: Xanthium orientale subsp. saccharatum, also known as common cocklebur, is an annual or biennial plant in the Asteraceae family, native to Asia and naturalized in other parts of the world. The plant is traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, including its ability to relieve fever and inflammation, as well as its antiseptic and antifungal properties. The health benefits of Xanthium orientale subsp. saccharatum include its potential to treat respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as its ability to lower blood pressure and improve digestion. The plant is often prepared for herbal use in the form of infusions, teas, or tinctures, which can be consumed orally to reap its medicinal benefits.
